item.title: Room for potential, but misses in the end.
item.content:encoded:
I am not an official reviewer so take what I say with a grain of salt! This is more of a "what feels wrong with this article and where can it be improved?"
I like the concept, but I feel like it was developed the wrong way/didn't explore everything.

  • Containment doesn't take into account that "SCP-4663 is capable of consuming all inorganic substances". (Unanswered Question)
  • SCP-4663-1 instances are alive even though they're made from thread that is created from inorganic substances? I don't think this really adds much except as an excuse to not experiment on SCP-4663-1 using 3D-printer-based experiments. (Doesn't Make Sense + Unanswered Question)
  • "physical changes and evolutions happen frequently," This feels more like an excuse for the Addendum which doesn't really add to the article since it's just random. What of the old SCP-4663 instances? I feel that an experiment/test log would be more appropriate than the Addendum, though that alone might not improve the overall article. (Doesn't Make Sense + Unanswered Question + Engagement Difficulty)
  • Even if you keep the Addendum, it pokes at a "what-if" that's not further explored. Maybe a post-incident report or purposefully recreating the incident in a controlled environment? (Unanswered Question + Engagement Difficulty)

Currently a 4/10
no vote

+ I hope this doesn't get me in trouble...
- I hope this doesn't get me in trouble...

…But that's the risk with speaking my mind on social forums.

if this was reddit i would link r/angryupvote arachnid based weaponry really got me

also in your above post spiderlaser cannon is just amazing and i want one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know how I didn't make that connection at first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. They did notice, that's how the foundation found out about them and got them all into containment. Could probably have done something with that but meh.

2. I'd say it's based on calculations of how quickly the reproduce and how long they've been in containment. The foundation doesn't have an exact number, but they can approximate it. It's also why after the incident they decided to restrict populations to 500,000, as that would give a significant amount of leeway before things got bad. As for why they started doing that at 1,000,000, I have no idea. Anomalous shenanigans or hive mind smarts or something.

3. Oh you know, guns that shoot spiders, whips made of a bunch of spiders strung together, whips made of spiders covered in guns that shoot spiders, silkswords, missile that explode into spiders, giant spiderlaser cannons, etc. Maybe even smaller spiderbots. Just all the spider weapons. All of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few things that break my suspension of disbelief.

1. These spiders are indigenous to three continents and reproduce rapidly. Seems likely someone would have seen them.

2. Is it really that easy to count a population of spiders to know that there were just over one million? And why does that cause this new behavior?

3. What exactly is "arachnid-based weaponry"? That phrase completely breaks the clinical tone for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall I really like this, though a couple caveats:

1. I'm not sure the incident described warrants the keter designation, if all it takes to prevent that is keep the colony small.
2. The bit about using it as trash disposal rubs me the wrong way, I'm not a big fan of casual use of SCPs for convenience
3. The language strays a bit away from the clinical tone when describing their escape attempt

+1

item.title: Love this
item.content:encoded: Super short and simple, but a really great concept. Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but do they have printers that eat, eaters that print, and everything in between?

item.title: So uhh yea whoop dee doo Xilas made another thing
item.content:encoded:
Ok so I don't know
This is a thing now
Read it, I guess

Hail giant spider printer robot thing
